{{Quote|The best way to enjoy one's success is to know how hard it is to earn. I didn't devour my hatchling-mates for nothing, you know.|Ssk Kahorr|<ref name="gas" />}}
The '''Cha'a''' were a small, orange-scaled, [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Species/Legends|species]], known for their cunning natures. They were part of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] society for many thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. One of the most famous Cha'a was the [[Merchant/Legends|Merchant]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]], [[Ssk Kahorr]], who lived during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. Other notable Cha'a included three [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] students who trained alongside [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] in the [[New Jedi Order]].

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Wabba1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Cha'a consumes food.]]
The Cha'a were a delicately scaled, [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Species/Legends|species]], of predominantly orange coloration. Above their wide-set, slitted [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a line of horns ran along the top of their heads and continued down their backs. They had long snouts with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] and a long, dexterous, tongue. The Cha'a were carnivorous, using their sharp teeth to consume live food shortly after they hatched. These teeth were sometimes also used against Cha'a hatchling-mates, determining the dominant members of each clutch of [[Egg/Legends|eggs]]. The Cha'a were bipedal, with each [[Limb/Legends|limb]] ending in three clawed digits.<ref name="gas" />

==Society and culture==
The Cha'a were a technically proficient species, designing [[Starship/Legends|starships]], such as the [[Cha'a drone ship]]s, and [[Droid/Legends|droids]], such as the [[message pod]]. The design of their drone ships was distinctive, with large ornate figureheads integrated into the design. One such ship was the ''[[Zeta Five]]'', which was destroyed on an untested [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]] that passed too close to the [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]].<ref name="gas" />

==History==
{{Quote|Wily, ambitious, untrustworthy—though the Cha'a can be counted on to look after their own interests.|Hovrak, Diversity Alliance advisor|<ref name="yjk8">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Diversity Alliance]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Ssk.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Lord Ssk Kahorr]]
The Cha'a were active in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] life from the times of the early [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Spread across the galaxy, Cha'a were known to live on urban worlds such as [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]] and well as the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] colony of [[Goluud Minor]].<ref name="gas" /> One powerful Cha'a in that era was [[Ssk Kahorr]], [[Lord/Legends|head]] of a [[Merchant/Legends|trading]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of drone ships. As a representative of his species, Kahorr was a ambitious individual, willing to take risks in his business, such as using the newly found [[Goluud Corridor]] hyperlane to access the mining colony. When the route proved unstable and destroyed his cargo-ship ''[[Zeta Five]]'', he had the [[Hyperspace Navigator's Guild]] representative [[Shodon Ko]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] and paid [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to hunt down the route's discovers, [[Gav Daragon|Dav]] and [[Jori Daragon]].<ref name="gas" /> Kahorr later confiscated the Daragon's starship, ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', and was killed when it was destroyed by the arrival of a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] armada in the opening salvo of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].<ref name="FSE 3">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]]</ref>

The Cha'a continued to be part of the [[galactic community]] into the administration of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], where three Cha'a hatchling-mates underwent [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] at [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. They helped defend the Praxeum from an [[Third Battle of Yavin|assault]] by the [[Second Imperium]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-aligned [[darksider]] training facility, by hiding in the murky jungle rivers to ambush [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]].<ref name="yjk6">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Jedi Under Siege]]''</ref> As a species, Cha'a were also targeted by the [[Diversity Alliance]] as possible recruits. [[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanen]], [[Adjutant Advisor]] [[Hovrak]] identified them as being wily, and ambitious, traits beneficial to the Alliance; however, the Alliance was defeated shortly after Hovrak's voiced his recruitment aim.<ref name="yjk8" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Kevin J. Anderson]] created the Cha'a for the [[Old Republic era]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith|''Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith'']] ([[1996]]) and [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''The Fall of the Sith Empire'']] ([[1997]]). Their only other appearances have been in the [[Star Wars: Young Jedi Knights|Young Jedi Knights]] series, co-written by Anderson and his wife, [[Rebecca Moesta]].
